sections.paragraph:
prop. וָאַל unused in Kal, to be foolish, i.q. אָוַל which see. [“The primary idea appears to be that of perverseness, i.q. עָוַל.”]

Niphal נֹואַלּ to be foolish, Num 12:11 Jer 5:4 to act as a fool, Isa 19:13. Jer 50:36, חֶרֶב אֶל־הַבַּדִּים וְנֹאָלוּ “the sword (is) upon the lying (prophets), and they shall act as fools” (comp. הֹולֵל Job 12:17 Isa 44:25).

II.

יאל

unused in Kal, kindred to אוּל, אִיל Arab. أَوِلَ to go before, to be first. [In Thes. the meaning of this root is given “properly to will, to wish.”]

Hiphil הֹואִיל to begin [“to wish, to will,” Thes.], Deu 1:5 Hos 5:11 Jos 17:12, וַיֹּואֶל הַכְּנַעֲנִי לָשֶׁבֶת בָּאָרֶץ “and the Canaanites began to dwell together (to set their feet) in the land.” Jud. 1:27 , 35 Jdg 1:35. Sometimes it is used with a more emphatic sense, to undertake, to endeavour, Gen 18:27, 31 Gen 18:31also of him who yields to the prayers of others, and does something, to be willing, to be pleased to do something, Jud. 17:11 19:6 , הֹואֶלּ־נָא וְלִין “be content now, and lodge.” Exo 2:21 2Sa 7:29 2Ki 5:23 Job 6:9, 28 Job 6:28. Construed with a gerund after it, Jos 17:12 Jud. 1:35 or with a finite verb sometimes joined by a conjunction ( Jud. 19:6 ), sometimes without one, ἀσυνδετῶς ( Deu 1:5 Hos 5:11). [Note in Ges. add. all these passages are referred to the idea of to will, to wish, either in the sense of undertaking what is wished, as Gen 18:27, 31 Gen 18:31 Jos 17:12 Jud. 1:27 , 35 Jdg 1:35 1Sa 17:39 or in the sense of being willing to yield to another, as Job 6:28 2Ki 6:3.]
